mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL indicates rising inflation expectations that reduce real discount rates and boost nominal equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in PAYEMS exceeding 100 thousand persons indicates weakening labor income growth that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines exceeding 100k persons over 2 months are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS and decreases in RSAFS indicate a monetary tightening cycle colliding with weakening consumer demand, jointly amplifying downside equity risk through compressed margins and reduced spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises MoM while RSAFS falls MoM
failure_condition: The joint condition is followed by positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to add marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus individual series in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in HOUST exceeding 50 thousand units over a three-month window indicates accelerating residential investment that drives early-cycle demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: HOUST increases exceeding 50k units over 3 months fail to coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances during post-recession recovery regimes, or the signal performs equally well in late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial profitability and capacity utilization that translates into positive earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month acceleration coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of weeks over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points indicates deteriorating household balance sheets and falling consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE month-over-month rises exceeding 0.2 percentage points are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered weeks over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in UNRATE and increase in INDPRO indicates a non-inflationary productivity boom that reduces unit labor costs and expands profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls MoM and INDPRO rises MoM
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of falling UNRATE and rising INDPRO do not produce positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS indicates weakening consumer spending that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month declines are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.
